## Summary

I found the bug. Here's the full picture:

### The Bug ‚Äî `src/utils.ts`, line 7

```typescript
// BUGGY: stops one short of the end
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

The `- 1` in the loop condition is the culprit. For an array of N items, the loop only processes indices `0` through `N-2`, permanently skipping the last element.

### The Fix ‚Äî one token removed

```typescript
// FIXED
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

### Impact on Tests (3 currently failing)

| Test | Status before fix | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `"should process all items in the array"` | ‚ùå FAIL | `"FOO"` is never pushed |
| `"should handle a single item"` | ‚ùå FAIL | `i < 0` is always false; returns `[]` |
| `"should handle two items"` | ‚ùå FAIL | Only `"A"` returned, `"B"` skipped |
| `"should return empty array for empty input"` | ‚úÖ PASS | Vacuously correct |
| `sum` tests (√ó2) | ‚úÖ PASS | Unaffected (`for...of` loop, no bug) |

After the one-character fix, all 6 tests will pass. Shall I proceed?
